samples.author: Nina Cash
samples.text:
New rooftop bar at the Nickel hotel on King street. It was my second time there so I came by after work to check out the vibes and it didn’t disappoint. The decor is charming and very chic, offers bar seats, some booths and sections indoor as well as some couches and outdoor tables. They have these fire-pit tables which was a nice touch with the cool nights. I sat at the bar this time and ordered a last word that was spot on. Snapper crudo was light and fresh, and the fried pastry was crispy, creamy and delicious. Their menu is small but had some good options across the board.
The prices were expected as it is a nice rooftop cocktail bar in downtown Charleston. Overall it was a nice time and I find it suitable for all types of groups for good vibes. 😎

samples.author: Patrick Vollmer
samples.text: This is the best rooftop bar in Charleston. It’s so put together and feels like you’re in Architectural Digest. A beautifully executed interior is matched by the service and drinks. As a Charlotte native, I won’t skip this place on my trips down. Just incredible.

samples.author: Gabriella Fielding
samples.text:
We ordered the shrimp, which was unfortunately incredibly fishy and not at all up to Charleston’s usual standard for fresh seafood. The tuna was good — fresh and well-prepared — and the Swiss chard ended up being the best thing we ordered.

Service left a lot to be desired. No one checked in after our food arrived, and one of the runners actually slammed the shrimp plate down without a word. The wine list also didn’t live up to expectations for the price point.

Portions were disappointingly small, leaving us still hungry by the end of the meal, and the outdoor tables were so low it made it difficult to comfortably enjoy the food. Overall, the food, service, and atmosphere all missed the mark — I wouldn’t go back.

samples.author: Katie Stone
samples.text: I had the worst experience with a male bartender last night. He was so rude, we waited extremely long for service, and when we asked for a second round of our drinks he said “well what was your drink? I serve lots of people, I don’t remember everything”. Very VERY unnecessary.

samples.author: Michael C. Fischbach
samples.text: Reminds me of rooftop bar 🍸  in DC. Decor is lush-chill. French Apps were delightful. My wife & I enjoyed the view.
